title: Greek Salad
excerpt: Ingredients 1 – 2 garlic cloves, very finely chopped 125 ml olive oil finely grated zest of 1 lemon juice of ½ a lemon (you'll need about 45 ml) 65 ml roughly chopped flat-leaf parsley 45 ml finely chopped oregano (or 15 ml dried) 5 ml salt 5 ml coarsely ground pepper a bit of sugar (only if you feel it's needed …
html:
Ingredients

  • 1 – 2 garlic cloves, very finely chopped
  • 125 ml olive oil
  • finely grated zest of 1 lemon
  • juice of ½ a lemon (you'll need about 45 ml)
  • 65 ml roughly chopped flat-leaf parsley
  • 45 ml finely chopped oregano (or 15 ml dried)
  • 5 ml salt
  • 5 ml coarsely ground pepper
  • a bit of sugar (only if you feel it's needed – it's not authentically Greek)
  • 6 smallish, well-ripened tomatoes, each cut into 6 wedges (tomatoes about the size of ordinary naartjies)
  • 1 medium white onion, cut into thin wedges
  • 375 ml Mediterranean cucumber slices (½ cm slices)
  • 250 ml pitted black olives
  • 2 – 3 whole wheels or blocks of the very best-quality feta cheese
  • a sprinkling of dried oregano
  • extra olive oil

Method

Put the garlic in a salad bowl and add the oil, lemon zest and juice, parsley, oregano, salt, pepper and, if you're using it, the sugar. Mix thoroughly.

Add the tomatoes, onion, cucumber and olives to the bowl and mix well. Cover the bowl and set aside at room temperature for at least an hour or longer for the flavours to develop.

Just before serving, open the bowl, give the salad one last stir and arrange the (whole) pieces of feta on top, sprinkling with dried oregano. Drizzle a little olive oil over the cheese and serve.

Serves 6

Easy!
